podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: The sharks almost walked away... #sharktank
podcasts.transcript: Her numbers scare [music] the sharks, but she shares a story that changes everything. A hundred and five dollars in cost. >> Yes. In cost? Not retail price? No, the cost What What do you sell it for? >> On average selling price is $320. That seems like a lot lot for a cotton dress. Kasida was born out of my deep love for chicken curry. How do you say it? Is it So, it's just chicken curry with an additional A, so it becomes chicken kari. >> Can I clarify? The women that are featured on the poster, they're doing the chicken [music] curry? >> Yeah, so right now there are six artisans who work for Kasida. Lucknow is the only place in this entire world where chicken kari can happen under the Indian intellectual property law. [music] Could you do it in Australia? >> No. No? >> Why? To preserve the craft, the artisans employment. Through Kasida, I truly hope to preserve a traditional form of handicraft [music] while empowering female artisans. Business is early stage. I've sold about 18 pieces. 18? Operating with that kind of cost structure, just with inventory, it can get really really capital intensive. And I just don't think I'm ready to do that right with you at this stage. So, for those reasons, I'm out. Just when rejection feels certain, wait for this. I will give you the full amount, but for 40% of your business. >> Katrina, would you consider 30%? >> Absolutely will. I would love Thank you so much.
